name: Route of the Monsanto Trails — circular route
name_original: Rota das Pista de Monsanto — percurso circular
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Ajuda are primarily found within the expansive Monsanto Forest Park, a significant green lung for Lisbon. This area features a mix of dense woodlands, open parkland, and varied topography with gentle hills. The trails often provide views over the city and the Tagus River, offering accessible outdoor experiences for families. Best family friendly hiking trails around Ajuda The most popular family friendly hiking route is Keil do Amaral Amphitheater – Monsanto Forest Park loop from Parque Florestal de Monsanto, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 21 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant walk through the park's green spaces. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Monsanto Trails — circular route, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route explores a wider section of Monsanto Forest Park, featuring diverse flora and gentle ascents. Local hikers also love the View of Ponte 25 de Abril – Keil do Amaral Amphitheater loop from Parque Florestal de Monsanto, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through the forested hills of Monsanto, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Ajuda is defined by dense woodlands, open parkland, and scenic viewpoints, with options for different ability levels within the family friendly category. The routes in Ajuda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1,500 reviews. More than 19,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ajuda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Ajuda?
A: Komoot offers over a dozen family-friendly hiking trails around Ajuda. These routes are specifically curated to be enjoyable for all ages, featuring manageable distances and gentle paths. You'll find a good mix of easy and moderate options to suit your family's preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in Ajuda?
A: The family-friendly trails around Ajuda, particularly within the Monsanto Forest Park area, generally feature varied terrain. You can expect well-maintained paths, some paved sections, and natural forest trails. While most are gentle, some routes might include slight inclines, offering a pleasant challenge without being overly strenuous for families.

Q: Are there any easy routes suitable for families with younger children or toddlers?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for families with younger children or toddlers. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40669063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Keil do Amaral Amphitheater – Monsanto Forest Park loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5 km trail that offers a pleasant walk through nature without significant elevation changes, making it ideal for little legs.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints we can see on these family hikes?
A: Many family-friendly hikes in Ajuda offer interesting sights. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1981857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes Claros Garden&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32220497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Ponte 25 de Abril – Keil do Amaral Amphitheater loop&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts cultural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Overseas Combatants 🏛️ Belém&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Christ the King (Santuário de Cristo Rei)&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen from various viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Absolutely! Many of the family-friendly trails around Ajuda are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380923121/route-of-the-monsanto-trails-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Monsanto Trails — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive experience of the park's natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the family-friendly trails in Ajuda?
A: The family-friendly trails around Ajuda are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 1500 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the peaceful natural surroundings of Monsanto Forest Park, and the accessibility for families with children.

Q: Is it possible to reach the trailheads using public transport?
A: Yes, many of the family-friendly trailheads, especially those within or near Monsanto Forest Park, are accessible via public transport from central Lisbon. Buses often serve the park's perimeter, making it convenient for families to reach the starting points without a car. We recommend checking local transport schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly options for family hikes in Ajuda?
A: Many trails in the Monsanto Forest Park area are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your furry family members along. It's always a good idea to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: What is the best season for family hiking in Ajuda?
A: Ajuda offers pleasant hiking conditions for families throughout much of the year. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are particularly ideal, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are best, while winter generally offers cooler, crisp days perfect for a refreshing walk.

Q: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or a picnic near the trails?
A: Yes, within and around Monsanto Forest Park, you'll find several spots suitable for picnics, often with designated areas. There are also cafes and kiosks, particularly near popular access points or attractions like the Montes Claros Garden, where you can grab a snack or a drink. Consider packing a picnic to enjoy amidst nature for a truly family-friendly experience.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water elements like lakes or fountains?
A: While Ajuda itself is not known for large natural lakes on its hiking trails, you can find beautiful garden lakes and fountains in nearby parks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5883754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of Parque da Paz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amália Rodrigues Garden Lake&lt;/a&gt; offer serene water features that can be incorporated into a family outing, often with pleasant walking paths around them.
tips:
On the Planalto Trail in the Monsanto Forest Park in Lisbon, you can find a series of wooden sculptures that are part of a very special visual and natural experience.
The sculptures are located in the cypress forest area, part of the Percurso Volta do Planalto, a circular trail of approximately 7 km.

Parque Eduardo VII
This famous park is named after British King Edward VII who visited the city in the twentieth century. Parque Eduardo VII is the largest park in the center of Lisbon. The park is built against a hill.

The façade of the palace is adorned with stone, shells, broken glass and porcelain. In addition, the dining room is decorated with portraits of members of the Portuguese nobility, painted by artists such as Domingos António de Sequeira. The chapel, dating from the 16th century, is the oldest part of the palace.
